compiled_state.title: As a brainstorm session participant, I can automatically discover relevant Confluence content during feature exploration without manually providing URLs
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: A dso:confluence-researcher sub-agent and its dispatch from brainstorm SKILL.md Phase 1.
**Why**: Agents currently plan features without access to institutional Confluence knowledge. This story surfaces relevant architectural decisions, API contracts, and domain guides automatically during the understanding loop — before the scrutiny pipeline runs.
**Scope**:
- IN: dso:confluence-researcher agent definition — accepts accumulated feature understanding context; auto-derives CQL keywords; queries GET /wiki/rest/api/search?cql=... across all accessible Confluence spaces via confluence-fetch.sh; returns top N page summaries ranked by CQL relevance score (N configurable via confluence.max_results, default 5)
- IN: Zero-results handling — emits "[confluence-research: no results for query: <keywords>]" to stderr and returns empty result set without failing the session
- IN: Timeout guard — curl --max-time 30 (or configurable) prevents a slow/unreachable Confluence instance from blocking the brainstorm loop
- IN: brainstorm SKILL.md modification — dispatches researcher at the end of Phase 1 understanding loop; result appended as "Confluence Research" section to the feature context; dispatch gated on CONFLUENCE_EMAIL and CONFLUENCE_API_TOKEN being set (skip silently when credentials absent)
- IN: Sub-agent nesting gate — when brainstorm itself runs as a sub-agent, skip researcher dispatch to avoid forbidden nesting depth
- OUT: roadmap integration (Story D)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, dso:confluence-researcher auto-derives CQL keywords from the feature understanding context, queries Confluence via the /wiki/rest/api/search?cql=... endpoint, and returns the top N summaries ranked by CQL relevance (N=5 by default, overridable via confluence.max_results)
  ← Satisfies: "A dso:confluence-researcher sub-agent is dispatched... auto-derives CQL keywords automatically, searches all accessible Confluence spaces, and returns the top 5 most relevant page summaries ranked by CQL relevance score"
- When this story is complete, when the CQL search returns zero results, the agent emits "[confluence-research: no results for query: <keywords>]" and the brainstorm session continues without error
  ← Satisfies: "When the CQL search returns zero results... the agent emits '[confluence-research: no results for query: <keywords>]' and continues without failing the session"
- When this story is complete, brainstorm SKILL.md Phase 1 dispatches the researcher at the end of the understanding loop; the researcher result appears as a "Confluence Research" section in the feature context used by the scrutiny pipeline
  ← Satisfies: "When /dso:brainstorm reaches the end of its Phase 1 understanding loop, a dso:confluence-researcher sub-agent is dispatched"
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Testing] CQL search must use a mock HTTP fixture — no live Confluence calls in tests; stub must return the 50-result response envelope format confirmed by atlassian-python-api
- [Reliability] sub-agent nesting gate: if brainstorm itself is dispatched as a sub-agent (e.g., within sprint), dispatching the researcher creates forbidden nesting — detect sub-agent context (Agent tool availability) and skip dispatch
- [Reliability] pre-dispatch credentials gate: brainstorm SKILL.md should check CONFLUENCE_EMAIL and CONFLUENCE_API_TOKEN before dispatching — skipping dispatch entirely when credentials are absent, rather than relying solely on confluence-fetch.sh's warning after dispatch
- [Integration] Verified: /wiki/rest/api/search?cql=... returns max 50 results per page; hard cap confirmed; for top-5 use case, first-page-only response is sufficient without pagination
- [Integration] Verified: response envelope has 'results' array and '_links.next' for pagination (atlassian-python-api, aashari/mcp-server-atlassian-confluence)

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.

comments.body: Consideration: [Reliability] Sub-agent nesting gate coverage gap — the primary detection method (Agent tool availability) fires correctly when brainstorm is dispatched as a Task-tool sub-agent, but does NOT fire when brainstorm is invoked inline by an orchestrator that passes through Agent tool access (e.g., debug-everything reading brainstorm SKILL.md inline). The gate implementation must handle both paths: check both Agent tool availability AND the orchestrator dispatch signal ('You are running as a sub-agent') described in CLAUDE.md. (taxonomy: consumer_impact)
comments.author: Claude

comments.body: Consideration: [Reliability] Brainstorm re-invocation idempotency — if a user re-invokes brainstorm mid-session or the scrutiny pipeline triggers a Phase 1 re-run, the researcher should be dispatched at most once per unique feature context (same session), not once per Phase 1 iteration. Duplicate dispatches produce redundant CQL API calls against the Confluence rate limit. Specify whether the researcher is dispatched on every Phase 1 loop exit or only once per session via a sentinel in ARTIFACTS_DIR. (taxonomy: implicit_shared_state)
comments.author: Claude

comments.body: Consideration: [Reliability] CQL syntax error handling — a zero-keyword CQL query (e.g., text ~ "") is syntactically invalid and returns an HTTP 4xx error, not zero results. The zero-results path ('emit warning and continue') handles empty result sets but not API errors caused by malformed CQL from sparse/non-English feature context. The researcher must distinguish between: (a) valid CQL + empty results → emit '[confluence-research: no results]', (b) invalid CQL / API error → emit '[confluence-research: CQL error: <message>]' and continue. (taxonomy: dependency_gaps)
comments.author: Claude
